content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic document truth route; stable document facts are extracted as claims, notes/sources keep richer context, and action items stay explicit-only.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DOC_DIGEST; v3 memory data model layered architecture; The `v3` memory data model is designed to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantic context, lifecycle/retention, and repairability by separating memory into layers: `Source` as evidence, `Entity`/`Claim` as graph-shaped semantics, `Note` as rich intermediate context, `Profile`/`Task`/`Episode` as operational projections, and `MemoryRun` as audit.; scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 1. Общая идея .. 2. Общие cross-cutting поля` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, describing the overall v3 memory data model architecture.
- claim hot-path:claim:<0> has_model_layer_component; In the v3 memory data model, Source is the evidence layer component.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 1 model layer decomposition
- claim hot-path:claim:<1> has_model_layer_component; In the v3 memory data model, Entity is part of the graph-shaped semantic layer.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 1 model layer decomposition
- claim hot-path:claim:<2> has_model_layer_component; In the v3 memory data model, Claim is part of the graph-shaped semantic layer.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 1 model layer decomposition
- claim hot-path:claim:<3> must_not_be_mixed_with; In the v3 memory data model, truth status and retrieval_state are different axes and must not be mixed.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 2.2 truth-status and retrieval-state semantics
- claim hot-path:claim:<4> answers_question; In the v3 memory data model, truth status answers whether knowledge is current, superseded, refuted, or past the end of its temporal window.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 2.2 truth-status semantics
- claim hot-path:claim:<5> answers_question; In the v3 memory data model, retrieval_state answers how often an item should participate in search and whether it is in hot, warm, or cold memory, archived, or hidden from ordinary retrieval.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 2.2 retrieval-state semantics
- claim hot-path:claim:<6> needed_for; In the v3 memory data model, scope_text is needed for explainability.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 2.1 field purpose
- claim hot-path:claim:<7> needed_for; In the v3 memory data model, scope_json is needed for retrieval and reconciliation.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 2.1 field purpose
- claim hot-path:claim:<8> has_cross_cutting_concern; The v3 memory data model has retrieval lifecycle as a cross-cutting concern.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 1 cross-cutting concerns
- claim hot-path:claim:<9> has_cross_cutting_concern; The v3 memory data model has scope as a cross-cutting concern.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 1 cross-cutting concerns
- claim hot-path:claim:<10> must_support_capability; The v3 memory data model must support reconcilable updates.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 1, imported data-model requirements
- claim hot-path:claim:<11> must_support_capability; The v3 memory data model must support temporal truth.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 1, imported data-model requirements
- claim hot-path:claim:<12> must_support_capability; The v3 memory data model must support lineage.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 1, imported data-model requirements
- claim hot-path:claim:<13> must_support_capability; The v3 memory data model must support typed memory.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md section 1, imported data-model requirements
- note hot-path:note:<1> DIRECTION; v3 scope_text and scope_json split; In the v3 memory data model, most memory items except raw `Source` should use a two-layer scope: `scope_text` for a short human-readable applicability description and `scope_json` as a machine-readable envelope for retrieval and reconciliation.; scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 2.1 scope_text и scope_json` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining the recommended scope pattern for memory items.
- note hot-path:note:<2> DIRECTION; Keep truth status separate from retrieval_state; The v3 data model treats `truth status` and `retrieval_state` as separate axes: `truth status` describes validity or supersession of knowledge, while `retrieval_state` controls search participation, memory temperature, archival state, and ordinary retrieval visibility.; scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 2.2 truth status и retrieval_state` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, defining separation of truth and retrieval lifecycle concerns.
- note hot-path:note:<3> DIRECTION; Usage fields support maturity, retention, and repair; For v3 notes, claims, tasks, and episodes, usage fields such as `use_count`, `last_used_at`, and `last_validated_at` are useful because they support maturity assessment, retention decisions, and repair workflows.; scope=Document section `02. Data Model / 2.3 Usage fields` in `agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md`, describing operational usage metadata for memory items.

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=Source; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<0>; type=existing; name=Source; reason=Mention refers to the existing memory data model concept Source.
- mention=Entity; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<1>; type=existing; name=Entity; reason=Mention refers to the existing memory data model concept Entity.
- mention=Claim; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<2>; type=existing; name=Claim; reason=Mention appears in Entity purpose as claims and matches existing memory data model concept Claim.
- mention=Task; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<3>; type=existing; name=Task; reason=Mention appears in Entity purpose as tasks and matches existing memory data model concept Task.
- mention=Note; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<4>; type=existing; name=Note; reason=Mention appears in Entity purpose as notes and matches existing memory data model concept Note.
- mention=Episode; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<5>; type=existing; name=Episode; reason=Mention appears in Entity purpose as episodes and matches existing memory data model concept Episode.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<6>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

Назначение: - хранить chat turns, tool outputs, imported notes, external records - быть первичным evidence layer - служить опорой для re-check и explainability Рекомендуемые поля: - `source_id: uuid` - `namespace_id: text` - `source_type: chat_turn | tool_output | imported_note | external_record` - `conversation_id: text | null` - `turn_id: text | null` - `speaker_role: user | assistant | tool | system | external` - `author_label: text | null` - `content_text: text` - `content_hash: text` - `observed_at: timestamptz` - `created_at: timestamptz` - `metadata_json: jsonb` - `retention_class: standard | short_lived | sensitive | imported` - `expires_at: timestamptz | null` - `deleted_at: timestamptz | null` - `embedding: vector | null` Инварианты: - source не должен silently изменяться - `observed_at` хранится отдельно от ingestion time - `content_hash` полезен для dedupe - delete policy должна быть явной --- ### 3.2 `Entity` Канонический узел памяти. Назначение: - объединять разные упоминания одного и того же объекта - быть якорем для claims/tasks/notes/episodes - поддерживать aliases и merge Рекомендуемые поля: - `entity_id: uuid` - `namespace_id` - `entity_type: person | agent | organization | project | repo | file | technology | product | location | concept | document | conversation | service` - `canonical_name: text` - `normalized_name: text` - `summary: text | null` - `status: active | merged | deleted` - `merged_into_entity_id: uuid | null` - `attributes_json: jsonb` - `first_seen_at: timestamptz` - `last_seen_at: timestamptz` - `created_at: timestamptz` - `updated_at: timestamptz` Отдельная таблица `entity_aliases`: - `alias_id` - `entity_id` - `alias_text` - `alias_normalized` - `source_id` - `confidence` - `created_at` Инварианты: - merge не удаляет историю - alias всегда указывает на текущий canonical entity - ошибочные merge должны быть обратимы через audit trail ---

Source metadata: source_kind=document; origin=pasted_document_section; document_type=MARKDOWN; source_ref=agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md; title=02. Data Model; heading=02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.1 `Source` .. 3.2 `Entity`; imported_at=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z
Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.1 `Source` .. 3.2 `Entity`
Document lines: 93-167

### 3.1 `Source`

Сырой источник знания.

Назначение:
- хранить chat turns, tool outputs, imported notes, external records
- быть первичным evidence layer
- служить опорой для re-check и explainability

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `source_id: uuid`
- `namespace_id: text`
- `source_type: chat_turn | tool_output | imported_note | external_record`
- `conversation_id: text | null`
- `turn_id: text | null`
- `speaker_role: user | assistant | tool | system | external`
- `author_label: text | null`
- `content_text: text`
- `content_hash: text`
- `observed_at: timestamptz`
- `created_at: timestamptz`
- `metadata_json: jsonb`
- `retention_class: standard | short_lived | sensitive | imported`
- `expires_at: timestamptz | null`
- `deleted_at: timestamptz | null`
- `embedding: vector | null`

Инварианты:
- source не должен silently изменяться
- `observed_at` хранится отдельно от ingestion time
- `content_hash` полезен для dedupe
- delete policy должна быть явной

---

### 3.2 `Entity`

Канонический узел памяти.

Назначение:
- объединять разные упоминания одного и того же объекта
- быть якорем для claims/tasks/notes/episodes
- поддерживать aliases и merge

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `entity_id: uuid`
- `namespace_id`
- `entity_type: person | agent | organization | project | repo | file | technology | product | location | concept | document | conversation | service`
- `canonical_name: text`
- `normalized_name: text`
- `summary: text | null`
- `status: active | merged | deleted`
- `merged_into_entity_id: uuid | null`
- `attributes_json: jsonb`
- `first_seen_at: timestamptz`
- `last_seen_at: timestamptz`
- `created_at: timestamptz`
- `updated_at: timestamptz`

Отдельная таблица `entity_aliases`:
- `alias_id`
- `entity_id`
- `alias_text`
- `alias_normalized`
- `source_id`
- `confidence`
- `created_at`

Инварианты:
- merge не удаляет историю
- alias всегда указывает на текущий canonical entity
- ошибочные merge должны быть обратимы через audit trail

---

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic document truth route; stable document facts are extracted as claims, notes/sources keep richer context, and action items stay explicit-only.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

Назначение: - хранить chat turns, tool outputs, imported notes, external records - быть первичным evidence layer - служить опорой для re-check и explainability Рекомендуемые поля: - `source_id: uuid` - `namespace_id: text` - `source_type: chat_turn | tool_output | imported_note | external_record` - `conversation_id: text | null` - `turn_id: text | null` - `speaker_role: user | assistant | tool | system | external` - `author_label: text | null` - `content_text: text` - `content_hash: text` - `observed_at: timestamptz` - `created_at: timestamptz` - `metadata_json: jsonb` - `retention_class: standard | short_lived | sensitive | imported` - `expires_at: timestamptz | null` - `deleted_at: timestamptz | null` - `embedding: vector | null` Инварианты: - source не должен silently изменяться - `observed_at` хранится отдельно от ingestion time - `content_hash` полезен для dedupe - delete policy должна быть явной --- ### 3.2 `Entity` Канонический узел памяти. Назначение: - объединять разные упоминания одного и того же объекта - быть якорем для claims/tasks/notes/episodes - поддерживать aliases и merge Рекомендуемые поля: - `entity_id: uuid` - `namespace_id` - `entity_type: person | agent | organization | project | repo | file | technology | product | location | concept | document | conversation | service` - `canonical_name: text` - `normalized_name: text` - `summary: text | null` - `status: active | merged | deleted` - `merged_into_entity_id: uuid | null` - `attributes_json: jsonb` - `first_seen_at: timestamptz` - `last_seen_at: timestamptz` - `created_at: timestamptz` - `updated_at: timestamptz` Отдельная таблица `entity_aliases`: - `alias_id` - `entity_id` - `alias_text` - `alias_normalized` - `source_id` - `confidence` - `created_at` Инварианты: - merge не удаляет историю - alias всегда указывает на текущий canonical entity - ошибочные merge должны быть обратимы через audit trail ---

Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.1 `Source` .. 3.2 `Entity`
Document lines: 93-167

### 3.1 `Source`

Сырой источник знания.

Назначение:
- хранить chat turns, tool outputs, imported notes, external records
- быть первичным evidence layer
- служить опорой для re-check и explainability

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `source_id: uuid`
- `namespace_id: text`
- `source_type: chat_turn | tool_output | imported_note | external_record`
- `conversation_id: text | null`
- `turn_id: text | null`
- `speaker_role: user | assistant | tool | system | external`
- `author_label: text | null`
- `content_text: text`
- `content_hash: text`
- `observed_at: timestamptz`
- `created_at: timestamptz`
- `metadata_json: jsonb`
- `retention_class: standard | short_lived | sensitive | imported`
- `expires_at: timestamptz | null`
- `deleted_at: timestamptz | null`
- `embedding: vector | null`

Инварианты:
- source не должен silently изменяться
- `observed_at` хранится отдельно от ingestion time
- `content_hash` полезен для dedupe
- delete policy должна быть явной

---

### 3.2 `Entity`

Канонический узел памяти.

Назначение:
- объединять разные упоминания одного и того же объекта
- быть якорем для claims/tasks/notes/episodes
- поддерживать aliases и merge

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `entity_id: uuid`
- `namespace_id`
- `entity_type: person | agent | organization | project | repo | file | technology | product | location | concept | document | conversation | service`
- `canonical_name: text`
- `normalized_name: text`
- `summary: text | null`
- `status: active | merged | deleted`
- `merged_into_entity_id: uuid | null`
- `attributes_json: jsonb`
- `first_seen_at: timestamptz`
- `last_seen_at: timestamptz`
- `created_at: timestamptz`
- `updated_at: timestamptz`

Отдельная таблица `entity_aliases`:
- `alias_id`
- `entity_id`
- `alias_text`
- `alias_normalized`
- `source_id`
- `confidence`
- `created_at`

Инварианты:
- merge не удаляет историю
- alias всегда указывает на текущий canonical entity
- ошибочные merge должны быть обратимы через audit trail

---
